page: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture utilizes a single thread per core and includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache. System memory totals 192.0 GB, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The instance includes 2835 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the D48pds_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The multi-core Geekbench score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top tier, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth measurements of 463.49 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 198.92 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Database operations on Passmark and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests show strong to top-tier results, while LLM inference speeds range from average for smaller models to weak for large 70B parameter models.

The ARM-based architecture of the D48pds_v6 offers high resource density and power efficiency, translating to a cost-effective profile for multi-threaded workloads. However, certain hardware tradeoffs exist. The processor lacks support for advanced vector and matrix extensions such as AVX, AVX-512, and AMX, which limits its efficiency for heavy mathematical modeling and deep learning tasks. While the server excels in memory-intensive operations and parallel processing, single-threaded tasks do not benefit from the same performance tier as multi-threaded execution.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, high multi-core throughput, and substantial local storage, this server is suited for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, and static web serving. It is also appropriate for Redis deployments, intermediate-scale text and image processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications that can leverage parallel ARM64 execution.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, 2835 GB of local storage, and is Premium Storage capable. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, as evidenced by high Geekbench multi-core and Passmark database scores. However, it lacks AVX and AMX instruction support, making it less suitable for heavy vector math. The qualitative cost efficiency of its ARM architecture makes it a strong option for multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 192 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, built for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores, balanced memory allocation, and high-capacity local storage for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
192 GB of system memory providing 4 GB per core
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing benchmarks
Lacks AVX, AVX-512, and AMX hardware instruction extensions
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation
